description: Comprehensive database of 3D printing print quality issues, causes, and fixes. Covers mechanical, slicer, firmware, and material factors.

issues.name: Not Sticking to Bed

causes.detail: Bed not properly leveled — nozzle too far from bed
Z-offset too high — first layer not squished enough
Bed temperature too low for material (PLA: 50-65°C, ABS: 90-110°C, PETG: 70-85°C)
Dirty or worn bed surface — clean with IPA, consider PEI/glass/textured sheet
First layer printing too fast — reduce to 15-25 mm/s
First layer height too thin — use 0.2-0.3mm for better adhesion
Part cooling fan too strong on first layers — disable for layers 1-3
Drafts or ambient temperature changes causing warping
No adhesion aid — use glue stick, hairspray, or brim

fixes: Re-level bed using paper test or BED_SCREWS_ADJUST
Lower Z-offset in 0.02mm increments until first layer is properly squished
Increase bed temperature by 5-10°C
Clean bed with isopropyl alcohol (IPA) before each print
Apply glue stick (for ABS/ASA) or use textured PEI sheet (for PETG)
Slow first layer speed to 20mm/s
Use a brim (5-10mm) for small or warp-prone parts
Disable part cooling fan for first 2-3 layers
Enclose printer for ABS/ASA to prevent drafts

issues.name: Warping / Corner Lifting

causes.detail: Material shrinks as it cools — ABS warps more than PLA
Insufficient bed adhesion at corners
Cold ambient air causes uneven cooling
Bed temperature uneven across surface

fixes: Use enclosure to maintain consistent ambient temperature
Add mouse ears/brim at corners
Increase bed temperature (5-10°C)
Use raft for large flat parts
Apply adhesive: glue stick for ABS, hairspray for PLA
Reduce infill density to minimize internal stress
Use heated chamber if available

issues.name: Under-Extrusion

causes.detail: Partial nozzle clog — debris or burnt filament
Extruder tension too loose — filament slipping
Filament diameter not calibrated — measure with calipers
Flow/extrusion multiplier too low in slicer
Print temperature too low for material
Printing too fast for hotend to melt filament
Heat creep — filament softens too high in hotend, causing jams
E-steps/rotation_distance not calibrated
Excessive friction in Bowden tube (for Bowden setups)

fixes: Perform cold pull to clear partial clog
Increase extruder tension or check gear for wear
Calibrate rotation_distance: mark 120mm, extrude 100mm, measure
Increase flow rate by 2-5% in slicer
Increase print temperature by 5-10°C
Reduce print speed by 20%
Check hotend cooling fan — ensure it runs continuously
Replace nozzle if worn (brass nozzles wear with abrasive filaments)
For Bowden: check tube for kinks, use Capricorn tubing

issues.name: Over-Extrusion

causes.detail: Flow rate/extrusion multiplier too high
Filament diameter set too thin in slicer
Temperature too high causing excess flow
Rotation distance too low (over-extruding)

fixes: Reduce flow rate by 2-5%
Measure filament diameter with calipers in 5+ spots, use average
Lower print temperature by 5-10°C
Calibrate rotation_distance

issues.name: Inconsistent Extrusion

causes.detail: Poor quality filament with inconsistent diameter
Worn or dirty extruder gear — slipping
Intermittent partial clog
Temperature fluctuations due to poor PID tuning
Filament spool binding or tangled
Pressure advance not calibrated

fixes: Use quality filament (Prusament, Hatchbox, eSun, Polymaker)
Clean extruder gear with brush; replace if worn
Perform cold pull or replace nozzle
Run PID_CALIBRATE for extruder and bed
Ensure spool rotates freely — use bearing spool holder
Calibrate pressure advance with TUNING_TOWER

issues.name: Stringing / Oozing

causes.detail: Retraction distance too short
Retraction speed too slow
Print temperature too high — filament too fluid
Travel speed too slow — filament has time to ooze
Coasting/wiping not enabled
Wet filament — moisture causes stringing and popping

fixes: Increase retraction distance (direct drive: 0.5-2mm, Bowden: 4-7mm)
Increase retraction speed (30-60mm/s direct, 40-80mm/s Bowden)
Lower print temperature by 5-10°C
Increase travel speed to 200-300mm/s
Enable coasting/wiping in slicer
Dry filament at recommended temperature for 4-8 hours
Enable z-hop only if needed (adds time but prevents nozzle drag)

issues.name: Clogged Extruder / Nozzle

causes.detail: Dust/debris in filament entered nozzle
Heat creep — filament softens in cold zone
Filament burnt from sitting at high temp too long
Gap between nozzle and heatbreak/PTFE tube
Printing at wrong temperature for material

fixes: Perform cold pull: heat to 170°C (PLA), push filament, cool to 90°C, pull firmly
Use nozzle cleaning needle
Replace nozzle (brass nozzles are consumable)
Check hotend cooling fan is always running when hotend is hot
Ensure PTFE tube is flush against nozzle (for PTFE-lined hotends)
Use filament dust filter

issues.name: Ringing / Ghosting / Echoing

causes.detail: Mechanical resonance/vibration from rapid direction changes
Acceleration too high for printer rigidity
Square corner velocity (jerk) too high
Loose belts amplify vibrations
Printer frame not rigid enough
Input shaper not configured or tuned

fixes: Tighten X and Y belts — should produce low bass note when plucked
Reduce acceleration (start at 1000-2000 mm/s²)
Reduce square_corner_velocity to 5.0
Enable and tune input shaper with ADXL345: SHAPER_CALIBRATE
Add frame bracing / stiffeners
Reduce moving mass (lighter toolhead)
Ensure printer on stable surface

issues.name: Visible Layer Lines / Z-Banding

causes.detail: Bent or dirty Z lead screw
Z-axis wobble from misaligned lead screw
Z-axis microstepping artifacts at non-optimal layer heights
Temperature fluctuations during print
Inconsistent extrusion flow

fixes: Clean and lubricate Z lead screw
Use flexible Z coupler (Oldham coupler)
Use optimal layer heights (multiples of full steps: 0.04mm steps for 1.8° motors with TR8x8)
Run PID_CALIBRATE for temperature stability
Check Z stepper Vref/current settings

issues.name: Blobs and Zits

causes.detail: Poor retraction settings — oozing at layer change
Visible Z seam alignment
Power loss recovery writing to SD — causes pauses
OctoPrint serial buffer starvation — pauses

fixes: Tune retraction (extra prime amount after retraction)
Set seam position to 'aligned' or 'sharpest corner'
Disable power loss recovery if using SD card
Increase OctoPrint serial buffer or print from virtual_sdcard
Enable coasting to relieve pressure before retraction

issues.name: Scars on Top Surface / Nozzle Drag

causes.detail: Z-hop disabled — nozzle drags during travel
Slight over-extrusion causes material buildup
Combing mode causes nozzle to travel through top surface

fixes: Enable z-hop (0.2-0.4mm)
Reduce flow by 1-2%
Set combing mode to 'not on outer surface' or 'within infill'
Enable ironing for smooth top surface

issues.name: Layer Shifting

causes.detail: Belt skipped teeth — too loose
Stepper current too low — motor stalls
Stepper driver overheating — thermal shutdown
Nozzle hit curled edge or blob — lost steps
Print speed/acceleration exceeds stepper torque
Pulley grub screw loose on motor shaft

fixes: Tighten belts — they should twang when plucked
Increase stepper current (TMC: increase run_current by 0.05-0.1A)
Check stepper driver cooling — add fan if needed
Enable z-hop to avoid collisions
Reduce acceleration and speed
Tighten all pulley grub screws with thread locker

issues.name: Layer Separation / Delamination

causes.detail: Print temperature too low — layers don't fuse
Layer height too tall for nozzle diameter
Part cooling too aggressive — cools before bonding
Material inherently prone to delamination (ABS, Nylon)

fixes: Increase print temperature by 5-10°C
Reduce layer height (max 75% of nozzle diameter)
Decrease part cooling fan speed
Use enclosure for warp-prone materials
Increase extrusion width slightly

issues.name: Weak Infill / Gaps in Infill

causes.detail: Infill printing too fast — underextrusion
Infill pattern too sparse for strength
Infill/perimeter overlap too low
Infill extrusion width too narrow

fixes: Reduce infill speed to match perimeter speed
Use gyroid, cubic, or honeycomb infill for strength
Increase infill/perimeter overlap to 25-30%
Increase infill extrusion width
Increase infill density (20-40% for structural parts)

issues.name: Pillowing / Gaps in Top Layers

causes.detail: Not enough top solid layers
Infill density too low to support top layers
Insufficient cooling — top layers sag into infill

fixes: Increase top solid layers to 5-6 minimum
Increase infill density to at least 15%
Increase part cooling fan for top layers
Add more perimeters for better support

issues.name: Dimensional Inaccuracy

causes.detail: X/Y/Z steps/mm not calibrated
Material shrinkage not accounted for
Extrusion width affects hole diameters
First layer squish affects Z height

fixes: Calibrate rotation_distance for all axes with precision gauge
Apply shrinkage compensation in slicer (ABS: ~0.5%, PLA: ~0.2%)
Print calibration cube (20mm) and measure with calipers
Use horizontal expansion/hole compensation in slicer
Set precise Z-offset for accurate Z height

issues.name: Elephant's Foot
symptoms: First few layers wider than rest

causes.detail: Bed too hot — bottom layers stay soft
Nozzle too close to bed on first layer
Weight of print compressing soft bottom layers

fixes: Reduce bed temperature by 5°C
Increase Z-offset slightly
Enable elephant foot compensation in slicer
Add chamfer to bottom edge in CAD

issues.name: Poor Bridging

causes.detail: Bridge speed too slow or too fast
Bridge flow rate not optimized
Insufficient cooling during bridging
Temperature too high — filament doesn't solidify quickly

fixes: Optimize bridge speed (20-30mm/s typically)
Set bridge flow ratio to 0.9-1.0
Run fan at 100% during bridges
Lower nozzle temperature for bridges by 5-10°C
Enable bridge detection in slicer

issues.name: Curling / Rough Overhangs

causes.detail: Insufficient part cooling
Printing overhangs too fast
Layer time too short — previous layer still soft
Temperature too high

fixes: Increase part cooling fan to 100%
Slow down for overhangs (50% of normal speed)
Set minimum layer time to 10-15 seconds
Lower print temperature
Add supports for angles > 45°

PLA.notes: Easy to print. Low warping. Not for high-temp applications. Biodegradable.

PETG.notes: Stronger than PLA. Less brittle. Sticks aggressively to PEI — use release agent.

ABS.notes: Strong and heat-resistant. Warps easily without enclosure. Fumes require ventilation.

ASA.notes: UV-resistant alternative to ABS. Similar properties but better outdoor durability.

TPU_FLEX.notes: Flexible. Print SLOW (15-30mm/s). Direct drive strongly recommended. Disable retraction if jamming.

NYLON_PA.notes: Very strong and durable. Extremely hygroscopic — MUST dry before printing (70°C, 6-12h).

PC_POLYCARBONATE.notes: Extremely strong, high-temp resistance. Requires all-metal hotend. Must dry thoroughly.

layer_height.description: Optimal layer heights for TR8x8 leadscrew (1.8° stepper, 16 microsteps)
layer_height.note: Use multiples of 0.04mm (full step) to avoid microstepping artifacts

nozzle_diameter.note: For 0.4mm nozzle: max layer 0.3mm, extrusion width 0.4-0.48mm

temperature_tower.description: Print a temperature tower to find optimal temperature for each filament

klipper_tuning_sequence.name: Flow Calibration
klipper_tuning_sequence.command: Print hollow cube, measure wall thickness

klipper_tuning_sequence.name: Pressure Advance
klipper_tuning_sequence.note: For direct drive: factor .005, Bowden: factor .020

klipper_tuning_sequence.name: Input Shaper
klipper_tuning_sequence.note: Or manual ringing tower: SET_VELOCITY_LIMIT MINIMUM_CRUISE_RATIO=0

klipper_tuning_sequence.name: Temperature Tower
klipper_tuning_sequence.command: Print test tower, inspect for best layer adhesion + least stringing

klipper_tuning_sequence.name: Retraction Tuning
klipper_tuning_sequence.command: Print retraction tower, find optimal distance + speed

methods: Use soluble supports (PVA/HIPS) for complex parts
support_removal.tips: Set support Z-distance to 1x layer height for easy removal, top contact Z-distance to 1-2x layer height

sanding.tips: Wet sanding for PLA reduces dust and heat. Start coarse, progress fine.

priming_painting.primer: Automotive filler primer (spray can) — fills layer lines

vapor_smoothing.method: Suspend part above liquid solvent in sealed container. Vapors smooth surface. 15-60 min exposure.
vapor_smoothing.safety: Highly flammable! Do outdoors or with ventilation. No ignition sources.

gluing_assembly.PETG: Super glue or epoxy — scuff surface first
gluing_assembly.ABS_ASA: Acetone welding — apply acetone to both sides, clamp together
gluing_assembly.general: Pin and socket joints for alignment, CA glue with accelerator

heat_treating.annealing_PLA: Heat to 80-100°C in oven for 30-60 min — improves strength and heat resistance
heat_treating.note: Parts will shrink slightly during annealing. Account for this in design.

troubleshooting_workflow.description: Systematic approach to diagnosing 3D print quality issues
steps: 1. IDENTIFY: What exactly is the problem? Take clear photos. Compare to reference images.
2. ISOLATE: Is the issue mechanical, slicer, firmware, or material?
3. PRIORITIZE: Fix the most fundamental issue first (bed leveling > extrusion > surface)
4. CHANGE ONE THING: Make a single change, reprint test object, compare
5. DOCUMENT: Record what worked. Update slicer profile. Save Klipper config.
